claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Remember that Child Benefit is assessed separately for each child.

**First question: How many children are you enquiring about, and what are their ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. 

I have one child - Alex, who is 12 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, who is 12 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, that's a good start. Now I need to ask you a few more questions:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to understand your responsibility for Alex:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means you're responsible for things like paying for their food, clothing, and other needs, or making decisions about their upbringing.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am responsible for Alex. I take care of all their needs - food, clothing, and everything else.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to check a few more things about your situation:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to ask about Alex's work situation:

**Is Alex working? If so, are they working full-time (meaning 24 hours or more per week on average)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now check if there are any other circumstances I need to consider:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the criteria:

✓ **Alex is 12 years old** - under 16, so qualifies as a child for Child Benefit purposes
✓ **Alex lives with you** - you meet the residency requirement
✓ **You are responsible for Alex** - you pay for their needs and care for them
✓ **You live in the UK** - you meet the residency requirement for the claimant
✓ **Alex is not working full-time** - they are not working 24+ hours per week
✓ **No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex** - there's no competing claim

Based on all the information you've provided and the UK Child Benefit eligibility rules, **you appear to be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

**Next steps:**
- You can claim Child Benefit online or by post
- You'll need Alex's birth certificate (or adoption certificate if applicable)
- You should claim as soon as possible, as Child Benefit can only be backdated for up to 3 months from when your claim is received
- The current rates are £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child

**Important note:** If you or your partner has an individual income over £50,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit. You can choose whether to receive the payments or just get National Insurance credits.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?
